trainees.name: Michael Wilson
trainees.current_summary: Senior trainee acting as a de facto coach. On May 12 walked Brandon through his first-ever spot one — drilled the leapfrog and up-and-over with him before the full-speed run, and Brandon picked it up cleanly enough for Jimmy to commend him directly. Continues to draw zero flags on his own work this session: clean satisfaction with Hailey, and chose to add a top-rope flip leg drop to his repertoire on the punching bag. Paired with Jack for Jack's first-ever promo and ran it cleanly. Refining a pile driver as a potential finisher and getting practice match reps in with guest workers.

trainees.name: Hailey Stokely
trainees.current_summary: Veteran trainee operating at near-trainer level. Back at the gym May 12 after missing May 8. Effectively co-ran the spot-one block — coached Jack through every unfamiliar piece (lockup-to-headlock transition, leapfrog form including where to bend and how low, hip toss form including foot placement and jump mechanics), then drilled the jumper role on the leapfrog with him (her first time as jumper) until she felt comfortable. Hit spot one flawlessly at full speed with Jack, then volunteered to run it a second time with Jeff to balance the odd numbers — also clean. Ran the second-rope hurricanrana on Jimmy on the turnbuckle and worked satisfaction with Michael. One self-inflicted clip on her first frog splash off the second rope — hand placement was slightly off. Caught DC's head dropping during the headlock, and shut down his promo cleanly with Jimmy when he crossed a personal-insult line. Currently locking in the hurricanrana and a satisfaction ahead of the next TAW show.

trainees.name: Jacob Justice
trainees.notes: Marked inactive May 12, 2026 after six consecutive absences from April 7 onward. Not expected to return to training.
trainees.current_summary: Selling visibly improved through early spring, but he hasn't been at a session since April 7 — six consecutive absences and counting. Confidence and pacing were the biggest gaps when he was active: flagged as timid and slow on rope-throw drills on April 7, and flagged as an injury risk for landing elbow-first on bumps. Hard to track progress with this much absence.

trainees.name: Tyler Skelton
trainees.current_summary: Big-frame trainee still learning how his height and size shape his ringwork. On May 8 he handled front rolls cleanly after some Cody form coaching, took the kick + back forearm combo with no notes, and got a Jaxson nod on a forearm that read light — clarified as a noise issue, not a contact issue. Got coached on punch contact during a live exchange with DC. Flip bumps and punches have been called out as good given his frame; the small package pin and rope-drill pacing remain places where his size needs technique to compensate. German suplex came together cleanly on April 24 with only minor pointers. Not at May 12.

trainees.name: Jay Taylor
trainees.notes: Removed from program April 14, 2026. Blackballed from local wrestling scene due to unsafe work, lack of attendance, refusal to accept criticism.

trainees.name: DC
trainees.notes: Promo flag: struggles to keep composure in promos and tends to escalate too hot. May 12, 2026 — crossed into personal insults during a heel/face spot with Hailey (called her a bitch), prompting Jimmy and Hailey to stop the promo on the spot and make clear that personal insults are a hard line.
trainees.current_summary: Form fundamentals continue to need polish, and May 12 doubled down on the pattern: stiff lockups again, head dropping during the headlock (caught by Hailey), drifting on the two-person rope-crossing drill (line-discipline call-out from Jimmy), reminder to always feed up to his right, and timing/coordination off on his first spot-one attempt with Nick — took two more reps and intervention from both Jimmy and Cody before it cleaned up. The bigger flag from this session is conduct, not technique: opposite Hailey in promos he tried to make her go first 'because she's a woman' despite her being the heel in the spot, then in his own promo got too heated and called her a bitch, crossing other personal lines as well. Jimmy and Hailey stopped the promo on the spot and made it explicit that personal insults are a line he cannot cross. He still drives his own technical refinement and follows through on outside-of-session work (one-on-one forearm session with Hailey on May 5), but May 12 is the first real character/conduct flag in his record and worth watching.

trainees.name: Dalton
trainees.current_summary: The most coaching-intensive trainee in the program right now. Despite roughly nine months in, fundamentals — selling, bracing, pacing, lockups, corner-pull form — keep getting flagged, and Hailey called him out directly on April 14 about how far behind his time-in-program he is. Injury risk has come up multiple times, including landing elbow-first on bumps and not bracing for impact. There's been progress in pieces (splash spot April 14, German suplex on the bag April 24, clean corner pull by the end of May 5), but the pattern is heavy correction every session. Hailey has stepped up as a regular partner and coach for him. Has now missed two consecutive sessions (May 8 and May 12).

trainees.name: Jack
trainees.notes: Age 14. Non-bumping restriction lifted May 5, 2026 — parent always present at training.
trainees.current_summary: Joined seven weeks ago at 14 and continues on a fast track. May 12 was a heavy spot-one session paired with Hailey — first time on the lockup-to-headlock transition, first time on the leapfrog as the jumper's base, and first time as the thrower on a hip toss. Hailey coached him through each piece (where to bend and how low on the leapfrog; foot placement, jump form, and getting out of his head on the hip toss); after the coaching he hit a great hip toss and the full-speed spot one was flawless. Also ran his first-ever promo paired with Michael — did well for himself. The pattern from prior sessions holds: brief coaching, then clean execution. Strength is still the ceiling on a few specific moves (couldn't lift the bag for a German suplex April 24).

trainees.name: Nick
trainees.notes: Jack's dad. Officially joined the program April 14, 2026.
trainees.current_summary: Five sessions in and continuing to execute cleanly. On May 12 took feed-up coaching after bumps and executed without issue afterward; only blip was a brief slip turning back to the ropes on the two-person rope-crossing drill with Hailey, otherwise smooth. Paired with DC on the first spot-one attempt of the session — the timing/coordination issues there were not specifically called out as Nick's. Earlier sessions: praised April 21 for a clean Japanese arm drag, April 24 for picking up the German suplex with no real friction, and May 8 for picking up Jimmy's front-forearm coaching cleanly and volunteering with Jack to demo punch-trading. Continues to be easy to coach — corrections picked up on the first try.

trainees.name: Brandon
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Has been training for a while and has worked a few matches at TAW.
trainees.current_summary: Two sessions in. May 12 was his first time running spot one — Michael walked him through the leapfrog and up-and-over before the full-speed run, and he picked the whole sequence up quickly enough for Jimmy to commend him directly. Two form flags this session: landing too far down on his tailbone during bumps (corrected after the comment) and drifting alongside DC on the two-person rope-crossing drill (line-discipline reminder from Jimmy). Also ran his first-ever promo paired with Jeff — neither knew how to proceed, so Jimmy stepped in and cut promos on both of them to kick it off. Joined from Cody McCulley's roster with TAW match experience already, so absorbing new spots quickly is consistent with that background.

trainees.name: Jeff
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Relatively new to training.
trainees.current_summary: Three sessions in and consistently easy to coach. May 12 added a clean run through spot one with Hailey — his first time on the sequence — after she volunteered to run it a second time to balance the odd number of trainees. First promo of his training paired with Brandon; neither knew how to proceed, so Jimmy stepped in and cut promos on both of them to demonstrate. Earlier reads: bump form and selling on the receiving end flagged positively May 5, clean live clothesline drill after walk-through, and on May 8 a clean front-roll set after Cody's coaching plus a lockup correction from Michael picked up immediately. Picks up corrections fast on the next rep.
